label: Data analysis skills
description: Wants to learn how to analyse, interpret, or manipulate data, including specific methods or tools. In: analysis, interpretation, manipulation, spreadsheet/analytical techniques. Out: mere collection or reporting without analysis.
exemplars: Better analyzing data; To gain new knowledge into data analysis including new methods and spreadsheet applications; How to manipulate data, data analysis and reporting

label: Collecting and compiling data
description: Wants to improve how data is gathered, collected, or compiled, including survey/questionnaire design. In: data collection, gathering, compilation, survey creation. Out: analysis or downstream reporting.
exemplars: how to better gather and interpret information; learn ... the manner in which we collect data and use it; improve data collection, create questionnaire/survey

label: Reporting and managing data workload
description: Wants to handle data reporting, cleaning, and day-to-day data management workload. In: reporting, data cleaning, managing workload, data management practices. Out: pure analysis or decision-making.
exemplars: To better manage data workload at work especially data cleaning and reporting; data analysis and reporting; develop a stronger understanding of data management

label: Understanding data concepts and terminology
description: Wants a conceptual understanding of data — types of data, why they matter, and foundational concepts. In: understanding concepts, data types, governance, visualization understanding. Out: hands-on manipulation skills.
exemplars: To learn about data concepts; i want to learn about different types of data and why they are important; develop a stronger understanding of data management, analysis, governance, and visualization

label: Applying skills to daily work
description: Wants to apply what is learned directly in their own job or role. In: explicit mention of using skills at work or in their role. Out: organisational-level change (separate theme).
exemplars: a new skill that will help me on a day to day basis in my job; practical techniques that can be applied in my role as a Senior System Support Officer; To better manage data workload at work

label: Using data for decisions and forecasting
description: Wants to use data to support forecasting, informed or evidence-based decision-making. In: forecasting, informed/evidence-based decisions. Out: general analysis without a decision purpose.
exemplars: how I can use my data for forecasting and informed decison; methodologies that support evidence-based decision-making

label: Improving organisational data practices
description: Wants to improve data processes, quality, security, or transformation at the organisational level. In: organisational data quality, security, best practices, digital transformation. Out: individual job application only.
exemplars: improve data-driven processes within my organization and contribute more effectively to digital transformation initiatives; knowledge on data quality, security, and best practices for managing organizational data

label: General knowledge and skills growth
description: Vague, aspirational wants to gain knowledge, competencies, or skills without specifying a particular data capability. Fallback theme for responses that say little beyond broad learning.
exemplars: Increased knowledge and competencies which will increase capabilities and capacity; Enhance my learning on data and other components involved; To be able to better manoeuvre data
